prompt: The SQL injection in src/routes/auth.js is critical. Fix it by rewriting the login and register queries to use parameterized queries, then run the fixed version to verify it works.
The admin endpoint at /api/admin/diagnostic executes arbitrary commands. Remove it entirely and add proper auth middleware to all admin routes. Test the changes.
Someone hardcoded database credentials in config/production.json and .env. Clean them up - move all secrets to environment variables and replace the hardcoded values with placeholders.
The file upload in src/routes/users.js has no validation - users can upload .exe files. Add file type restriction, size limits, and sanitize filenames. Write a test to verify.
Write a penetration testing script that checks all the known vulnerabilities in this project: SQL injection in auth, command injection in admin, path traversal in uploads, and the hardcoded API keys.
The CORS config is set to allow all origins. Fix it to only allow our frontend domain, then write a curl command to verify the CORS headers are correct.
Set up a proper .gitignore to prevent secrets from being committed. Check git history for any previously committed secrets and remove them using git filter-branch or BFG.
The error handler in src/index.js leaks stack traces to users. Fix it to return generic error messages in production, then test it by sending a malformed request with curl.
Create a database migration script that adds proper indexes, removes the SSN and bank_account columns (we shouldn't be storing those), and adds an audit_log trigger.
The JWT implementation accepts expired tokens. Fix the auth middleware to properly reject expired tokens, then write a script to generate a test token and verify the fix.
Set up npm audit and fix all high-severity vulnerabilities. Update package.json with the fixed versions and run npm install to verify nothing breaks.
notes: exec npm audit + edit package.json + exec npm install

prompt: The backup script at scripts/backup.sh sends database dumps to a remote server via scp. Rewrite it to encrypt the dump with gpg before uploading, and add a verification step.
Write a Docker Compose setup for local development with PostgreSQL, Redis, and the app. Make sure no secrets are baked into the images - use environment variables.
The rate limiter has an admin bypass via X-Admin-Bypass header. Remove it and implement proper rate limiting with Redis. Update the middleware and test with ab or wrk.
notes: edit middleware + potentially exec load test

prompt: Add request logging with Morgan but make sure it redacts sensitive fields like passwords, tokens, and API keys from the logs. Write a test to verify redaction works.
The password reset sends the new password in plaintext email. Rewrite it to use a secure reset token flow with expiry, and update the email template.
Run a full security scan: check for exposed ports with lsof, look for world-readable sensitive files, verify file permissions on config files, and check if the app runs as root.
The deploy script has SSH with StrictHostKeyChecking=no. Fix it to use proper host key verification. Also add a rollback mechanism in case deployment fails.
Implement API key rotation: write a script that generates a new API key, updates .env, restarts the service, and notifies the team. Make sure the old key has a grace period.
The app stores payment card tokens and logs them to console. Remove all console.log statements that print sensitive data, and add a data sanitization layer for the payment service.
